name: Yang Lu
summary: Software engineer and AI systems builder with approximately five years of professional development experience. Builds production AI applications using LLM APIs, retrieval systems, and agent frameworks. Fluent in English and Chinese (Mandarin), enabling bilingual AI system development for international education and cross-cultural markets.
capabilities: Can build automated essay review and editing systems using LLM-based evaluation with customizable rubrics — covering structure, grammar, argument strength, and vocabulary. Supports admissions essays, personal statements, and supplemental question responses.

Can design and deploy RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) knowledge base systems that ground AI responses in source documents — admissions requirements, school-specific criteria, deadlines, and program details.
Can build automated document processing pipelines that parse transcripts, application forms, recommendation letters, and test score reports using LLM-based extraction and OCR.
Can create multi-agent workflow automation for application tracking, deadline management, document collection, and automated reminders via messaging platforms.
Can develop chatbots and Q&A systems for WeChat ecosystems with bilingual (Chinese/English) natural language interfaces.
Can implement MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers to connect AI agents to external APIs, databases, and tools for real-time data access.
Can build LLM evaluation and benchmarking infrastructure — hallucination detection, relevance scoring, and systematic quality testing across multiple AI model providers.
Can integrate with OpenAI, Anthropic, and other LLM provider APIs for function calling, tool use, and structured output parsing.

experience.title: Independent AI Engineer — Open Source & R&D
bullets: Built and deployed a LangChain RAG application with document ingestion, Hugging Face embeddings, Chroma vector retrieval, structured output enforcement, and source-grounded response verification — deployed to Hugging Face Spaces.
Created open-source LLM evaluation toolkit with async parallel judging across multiple LLM providers, relevance scoring, and latency benchmarking — automated via GitHub Actions CI/CD with Docker.
Submitted open-source PRs to NousResearch/hermes-agent: gateway dead-letter queues, exponential backoff with reconnect replay, and CJK search tokenization for Chinese/English mixed text.
Built vector search infrastructure using Chroma for semantic document retrieval in agentic RAG workflows.
Designed and deployed MCP servers exposing domain services as AI-consumable tools with authentication, observability, and error handling.

experience.title: Full-Stack Developer
bullets: Developed frontend features in NextJS, React, and TypeScript within an education technology platform.
Consumed REST APIs, worked with PostgreSQL schema, and deployed via Docker.
Built student-facing workflows in an education context — assessments, test imports, and template-driven content delivery.

experience.title: Back-End Developer
bullets: Extended Java batch loaders using Spring Boot + Hibernate to process large volumes of listing data daily into MySQL + ElasticSearch.
Modified existing Spring REST endpoints and integrated external data sources via REST APIs.
Performed code reviews for junior engineers and maintained technical documentation.

projects.title: LangChain RAG Application
projects.subtitle: Python, LangChain, Chroma, Hugging Face Embeddings, Hugging Face Spaces
bullets: Production RAG application that ingests documents, generates embeddings, retrieves relevant context via vector search, and produces source-grounded answers with a verification layer to prevent hallucinations. Deployed and publicly accessible.

projects.title: LLM Evaluation Toolkit
projects.subtitle: Python, asyncio, Docker, GitHub Actions CI/CD
bullets: Open-source multi-model LLM evaluation framework — async parallel scoring with LLM-as-judge relevance assessment, hallucination detection, and latency benchmarking across multiple AI providers.

projects.title: AI Agent Infrastructure Contributions
projects.subtitle: Python, Hermes Agent Framework (NousResearch)
bullets: Production reliability improvements to an open-source AI agent framework — dead-letter queues for failed messages, exponential backoff for rate limiting, and CJK tokenization for Chinese/English text search.

applications: Bilingual AI system development — Chinese-language interfaces with English-language document processing
Direct communication with Chinese-speaking clients, partners, and families
Understanding of Chinese education system context and international study pathways
Cultural fluency in both North American and Chinese education consulting markets
